dict.string: Configures Visual Studio Code configuration preferences
Optional. A human-readable description of this payload. This description is shown on the Detail screen.
A human-readable name for the profile payload. This name is displayed on the Detail screen. It does not have to be unique.
A unique identifier for the payload, dot-delimited. Usually root PayloadIdentifier+subidentifier
A reverse-DNS-style identifier for the specific payload. It is usually the same identifier as the root-level PayloadIdentifier value with an additional component appended.
The type of the payload, a reverse dns string.
A globally unique identifier for the payload. The actual content is unimportant, but it must be globally unique. In macOS, you can use uuidgen to generate reasonable UUIDs.
The version of the whole configuration profile.

The version number of the individual payload. A profile can consist of payloads with different version numbers. For example, changes to the VPN software in iOS might introduce a new payload version to support additional features, but Mail payload versions would not necessarily change in the same release.
This value describes the issuing organization of the profile, as displayed to the user
When enabled, chat agents can use browser tools to open and interact with pages in the Integrated Browser.
When enabled, chat agents can use browser tools to open and interact with pages in the Integrated Browser.

Allowed domains for network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser). Applies when #chat.agent.networkFilter# or #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled. When #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is set to allowNetwork, all domains are allowed. Supports wildcards like *.example.com. When both allowed and denied lists are empty, all domains are blocked. Denied domains (see #chat.agent.deniedNetworkDomains#) take precedence.

Allowed domains for network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser). Applies when #chat.agent.networkFilter# or #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled. When #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is set to allowNetwork, all domains are allowed. Supports wildcards like *.example.com. When both allowed and denied lists are empty, all domains are blocked. Denied domains (see #chat.agent.deniedNetworkDomains#) take precedence.

Denied domains for network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser). Applies when #chat.agent.networkFilter# or #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled. This does not apply when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is set to allowNetwork. Takes precedence over #chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains#. Supports wildcards like *.example.com.

Denied domains for network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser). Applies when #chat.agent.networkFilter# or #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled. This does not apply when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is set to allowNetwork. Takes precedence over #chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains#. Supports wildcards like *.example.com.
Enable using tools contributed by third-party extensions.
Enable using tools contributed by third-party extensions.
When enabled, agent mode can be activated from chat and tools in agentic contexts with side effects can be used.
When enabled, agent mode can be activated from chat and tools in agentic contexts with side effects can be used.

When enabled, network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser) is restricted according to #chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains# and #chat.agent.deniedNetworkDomains#. Domain filtering is also applied to those tools when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.

When enabled, network access by agent tools (fetch tool, integrated browser) is restricted according to #chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains# and #chat.agent.deniedNetworkDomains#. Domain filtering is also applied to those tools when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.

Setting this policy to a non-empty list activates the Approved Account gate: all AI features are disabled until the user signs into a GitHub account whose organizations intersect this list AND the account-side policy data has resolved. Comparison is case-insensitive. Use '*' as a wildcard to accept any signed-in GitHub or GHE account (use this for GHE deployments where the organization list is not surfaced).

Setting this policy to a non-empty list activates the Approved Account gate: all AI features are disabled until the user signs into a GitHub account whose organizations intersect this list AND the account-side policy data has resolved. Comparison is case-insensitive. Use '*' as a wildcard to accept any signed-in GitHub or GHE account (use this for GHE deployments where the organization list is not surfaced).
Controls whether chat hooks are executed at strategic points during an agent's workflow. Hooks are loaded from the files configured in #chat.hookFilesLocations#.
Controls whether chat hooks are executed at strategic points during an agent's workflow. Hooks are loaded from the files configured in #chat.hookFilesLocations#.
Controls access to installed Model Context Protocol servers.
Controls access to installed Model Context Protocol servers.

array.string: Allows access to MCP servers installed from the registry that VS Code is connected to.
Allow access to any installed MCP server.

dict.string: Enable agent plugin integration in chat.
Enable agent plugin integration in chat.

Global auto approve also known as "YOLO mode" disables manual approval completely for all tools in all workspaces, allowing the agent to act fully autonomously. This is extremely dangerous and is never recommended, even containerized environments like Codespaces and Dev Containers have user keys forwarded into the container that could be compromised. This feature disables critical security protections and makes it much easier for an attacker to compromise the machine. Note: This setting only controls tool approval and does not prevent the agent from asking questions. To automatically answer agent questions, use the #chat.autoReply# setting.

Global auto approve also known as "YOLO mode" disables manual approval completely for all tools in all workspaces, allowing the agent to act fully autonomously. This is extremely dangerous and is never recommended, even containerized environments like Codespaces and Dev Containers have user keys forwarded into the container that could be compromised. This feature disables critical security protections and makes it much easier for an attacker to compromise the machine. Note: This setting only controls tool approval and does not prevent the agent from asking questions. To automatically answer agent questions, use the #chat.autoReply# setting.

Controls which tools are eligible for automatic approval. Tools set to 'false' will always present a confirmation and will never offer the option to auto-approve. The default behavior (or setting a tool to 'true') may result in the tool offering auto-approval options.

Controls which tools are eligible for automatic approval. Tools set to 'false' will always present a confirmation and will never offer the option to auto-approve. The default behavior (or setting a tool to 'true') may result in the tool offering auto-approval options.
Chat Tools Eligible For Auto Approval
Enable Claude Agent sessions in VS Code. Start and resume agentic coding sessions powered by Anthropic Claude Agent SDK directly in the editor. Uses your existing Copilot subscription.
Enable Claude Agent sessions in VS Code. Start and resume agentic coding sessions powered by Anthropic Claude Agent SDK directly in the editor. Uses your existing Copilot subscription.
Whether to enable next edit suggestions (NES). NES can propose a next edit based on your recent changes.
Whether to enable next edit suggestions (NES). NES can propose a next edit based on your recent changes.
Enables code review on current selection.
Enables code review on current selection.
Enable session sync to GitHub.com for cross-device Copilot session history. When disabled by organization policy, session data is kept local only.
Enable session sync to GitHub.com for cross-device Copilot session history. When disabled by organization policy, session data is kept local only.
When enabled, hides the Edit mode from the chat mode picker.
When enabled, hides the Edit mode from the chat mode picker.
Configure the MCP Gallery service URL to connect to
Configure the MCP Gallery service URL to connect to

Specify a list of extensions that are allowed to use. This helps maintain a secure and consistent development environment by restricting the use of unauthorized extensions. More information: https://aka.ms/vscode/enterprise/extensions/allowed

Specify a list of extensions that are allowed to use. This helps maintain a secure and consistent development environment by restricting the use of unauthorized extensions. More information: https://aka.ms/vscode/enterprise/extensions/allowed
Configure the Marketplace service URL to connect to
Configure the Marketplace service URL to connect to

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run inside the sandbox are auto-approved. When disabled, the run in terminal tool uses the existing approval flow. This applies only when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run inside the sandbox are auto-approved. When disabled, the run in terminal tool uses the existing approval flow. This applies only when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.
Chat Agent Sandbox Allow Auto Approve

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands can run outside the sandbox after user confirmation when a sandboxed command fails or when sandbox restrictions would block the command. This applies only when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands can run outside the sandbox after user confirmation when a sandboxed command fails or when sandbox restrictions would block the command. This applies only when #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# is enabled.
Chat Agent Sandbox Allow Unsandboxed Commands

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run outside the sandbox are auto-approved. This applies only when both #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# and #chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands# are enabled.

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run outside the sandbox are auto-approved. This applies only when both #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# and #chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands# are enabled.
Chat Agent Sandbox Auto Approve Unsandboxed Commands

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run outside the sandbox are auto-approved. This applies only when both #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# and #chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands# are enabled.

Controls whether agent mode terminal commands that run outside the sandbox are auto-approved. This applies only when both #chat.agent.sandbox.enabled# and #chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands# are enabled.

array.string: Disable sandboxing for agent mode tools.
Enable sandboxing for agent mode tools.
Enable sandboxing for agent mode tools and allow all network domains.

dict.string: Controls whether to allow auto approval in the run in terminal tool.
Controls whether to allow auto approval in the run in terminal tool.
Chat Tools Terminal Enable Auto Approve
Enable feedback mechanisms such as the issue reporter, surveys, and other feedback options.
Enable feedback mechanisms such as the issue reporter, surveys, and other feedback options.

Controls Visual Studio Code telemetry, first-party extension telemetry, and participating third-party extension telemetry. Some third party extensions might not respect this setting. Telemetry helps us better understand how Visual Studio Code is performing, where improvements need to be made, and how features are being used. Read more about the [data we collect](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/telemetry) and our [privacy statement](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/privacy/privacystatement). A full restart restart of the application is necessary for crash reporting changes to take effect.

array.string: Sends usage data, errors, and crash reports.
Sends general error telemetry and crash reports.

dict.string: Configure whether you receive automatic updates. Requires a restart after change. The updates are fetched from a Microsoft online service.
Configure whether you receive automatic updates. Requires a restart after change. The updates are fetched from a Microsoft online service.

array.string: Disable automatic background update checks. Updates will be available if you manually check for updates.
Check for updates only on startup. Disable automatic background update checks.
Enables automatic update checks. Code will check for updates automatically and periodically.
